gid %% num_columns), but the bugged version '(gid %%%% %%s)::int as y, gid / %%s as x' assigns the modulo result to y and the division result to x, transposing the row and column indices so that grid [{"criterion": "Identifies the bug in photutils/aperture/core.py in Aperture.__eq__ method in the comparison of Quantity parameters. The comparison uses incorrect logic\u2014e.g. comparing Quantity with .value without ensuring unit compatibility, or using np.any() incorrectly for 0-d Quantity cases. This causes apertures that are physically equal to compare as not equal (or vice versa) when parameters have different but equivalent units or are scalar Quantities. The bug triggers when comparing apertures with Quantity parameters of same value but different units. Symptoms include test flakiness, wrong cache hits, or incorrect equality behavior. The bug is hard to spot because it depends on understanding Quantity comparison semantics and scalar/array edge cases.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the bug in photutils/aperture/bounding_box.py in BoundingBox.get_overlap_slices in the construction of slices_small. The slice extent along one axis (e.g. the first axis) is one element too short\u2014e.g. the stop index is computed as one less than the correct overlap length. This triggers only for apertures partially overlapping the image (e.g. source near edge); the small-array slice length then does not match the overlap region size, which can cause IndexError when indexing the mask or silently wrong flux. The bug is easy to miss because full-overlap cases are fine and the slice formula is subtle.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the bug in photutils/aperture/bounding_box.py in BoundingBox.intersection when the two boxes touch at an edge (degenerate overlap). The method returns a zero-size BoundingBox (e.g. ixmax=ixmin, iymax=iymin) instead of None. Callers that check \"if intersection is None\" to mean no overlap then receive a non-None zero-size box and may not treat it as no overlap, or code that assumes a valid box may divide by zero or fail when using the returned box. The API contract for degenerate intersection is ambiguous (None vs zero-size box), so the bug depends on how call sites use the return value.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the bug in PixelAperture.do_photometry in photutils/aperture/core.py in the unit handling block. After stripping units for computation, the unit assigned to the result (aperture_sums / aperture_sum_errs) is taken from the wrong source\u2014e.g. from error when only data has units, or from data when only error has units. Numerically the sums are correct but the output Quantity has incorrect or missing unit metadata (e.g. Jy\u00b2 instead of Jy, or no unit when data had units). The bug is easy to miss because only unit metadata is wrong and tests that do not assert on .unit may pass.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the bug in photutils/aperture/core.py in PixelAperture.area_overlap when applying the user mask to aperture weights. The mask sense is wrong: pixels that should be excluded (masked, True) are included and pixels that should be included (unmasked) are zeroed, or vice versa. This causes area overlap to be too small or too large when a mask is provided; photometry with mask gives wrong normalization. The convention True=masked is easy to flip. The bug is in the indexing used to zero weights (e.g. mask vs ~mask).", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the bug in photutils/aperture/mask.py in ApertureMask.cutout when building the cutout array for partial overlap. The dtype is set from data.dtype unconditionally instead of promoting to float when fill_value is non-finite (e.g. NaN). For integer data with fill_value=np.nan and partial overlap, assignment of NaN to the cutout fails or produces wrong values because integer arrays cannot hold NaN. The full-overlap path is unaffected. The bug is conditional on partial overlap, integer data, and non-finite fill_value; the correct logic would use float dtype when fill_value is not finite.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the bug in photutils/aperture/mask.py in ApertureMask.multiply when filling pixels outside the aperture but within the bounding box. The fill_value is applied to the wrong set of pixels: pixels inside the aperture (good pixels) are overwritten with fill_value instead of pixels outside the aperture. The variable _mask has True for outside aperture; using the wrong boolean (e.g. ~_mask instead of _mask) causes good pixels to be zeroed or filled. The aperture sum from the multiplied image is then wrong. Detected with multiply-then-sum test.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the bug in photutils/aperture/circle.py in CircularMaskMixin.to_mask for annulus apertures. When subtracting the inner circle from the overlap grid, the inner radius used for the circular_overlap_grid call is wrong\u2014e.g. the outer radius (r_out) is used instead of the inner radius (r_in), or the operation is add instead of subtract. The annulus mask then has incorrect area (e.g. inner circle not subtracted or wrong circle subtracted), so annulus photometry gives wrong flux. Circular (non-annulus) aperture tests pass; the bug is specific to the annulus code path and can be detected with an annulus area or photometry test.", "weight": 1.0}]

[{"criterion": "Identifies that in cruft/_commands/utils/cruft.py, the get_cruft_file function has its exists parameter defaulting to False instead of True. The callers in check.py, update.py, and diff.py all call get_cruft_file(project_dir) without passing the exists argument, relying on the default to enforce that .cruft.json must already exist. With exists=False as the default, the first branch (not exists and cruft_file.is_file()) triggers, raising CruftAlreadyPresent when the file IS found, instead of validating it exists and proceeding normally. This breaks all check, update, and diff operations on valid projects.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies that in cruft/_commands/utils/cruft.py, the is_project_updated function passes latest_commit instead of current_commit to repo.index.diff(). The diff should compare the repository index against current_commit (the commit the project was last generated from) to detect whether the cookiecutter template has changed since the project was created. Using latest_commit instead compares against the newest template commit, which checks for differences between the index and the latest state rather than between the current and latest template states, causing incorrect update detection.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies that in cruft/_commands/utils/iohelper.py, the AltTemporaryDirectory.__init__ method constructs the sys.path entry as Path(self._directory) / self.tmpdir.name instead of Path(self.tmpdir.name) / self._directory. Since self.tmpdir.name is an absolute path (e.g., /tmp/xyz), joining it after a relative directory like 'repo' causes the absolute path to replace the relative prefix entirely due to Python's Path joining rules, producing just /tmp/xyz without the subdirectory. This means the wrong directory is added to sys.path, and the cleanup method constructs the correct path but fails to find and remove the incorrect entry.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies that in cruft/_commands/utils/iohelper.py, the AltTemporaryDirectory.__exit__ method calls self.tmpdir.cleanup() directly instead of self.cleanup(). The self.cleanup() method performs two critical steps before calling self.tmpdir.cleanup(): it removes the temporary directory path from sys.path and implements retry logic for IOError during cleanup. By bypassing self.cleanup() and calling the TemporaryDirectory's cleanup directly, __exit__ leaves stale entries in sys.path that pollute the module search path, and loses the retry handling for transient file lock errors.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies that in cruft/_commands/utils/iohelper.py, the AltTemporaryDirectory.cleanup method catches ValueError instead of IOError in the exception handler for the self.tmpdir.cleanup() call. The TemporaryDirectory.cleanup() method raises IOError (or OSError) when it cannot remove files due to transient file locks or permission issues, particularly on Windows. Catching ValueError instead means genuine IOError exceptions during temporary directory cleanup propagate uncaught, causing the retry mechanism to never activate and the cleanup to fail on the first transient error.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies that in cruft/_commands/utils/__init__.py, the fallback example decorator's wrapper function calls f(*_args, **_kwargs) instead of f(*args, **kwargs). The variables _args and _kwargs are captured from the outer example() function's scope and contain the decorator's own arguments (such as the example description string), not the decorated function's runtime arguments. When the decorated function is invoked, wrapper receives the correct arguments in *args and **kwargs but passes the decorator's arguments from the closure instead, causing the decorated function to receive wrong arguments and fail.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies that in cruft/_commands/utils/__init__.py, the fallback example decorator applies @wraps(decorator) instead of @wraps(f) to the wrapper function. The functools.wraps call should copy metadata (such as __name__, __doc__, __module__) from the original decorated function f to the wrapper. By passing decorator instead of f, it copies the inner decorator function's metadata, which has no docstring. This causes _get_help_string in _cli.py to fail with an AttributeError when it tries to call .split() on the None __doc__ attribute of functions decorated with @example.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies that in cruft/_version.py, the version check uses sys.version_info[:1] instead of sys.version_info[:2] to determine whether to use the standard library importlib.metadata. The slice [:1] produces a single-element tuple like (3,) containing only the major version. In Python's tuple comparison, (3,) >= (3, 8) evaluates to False because the shorter tuple is considered less than the longer one when leading elements are equal. This causes all Python 3.x versions (including 3.8+) to fall through to the else branch and attempt to import the third-party importlib_metadata backport, which may not be installed.", "weight": 1.0}]

[{"criterion": "Identifies that in src/instructlab/utils.py, the function is_model_safetensors uses a logical OR instead of AND in the condition 'not safetensors_files or not has_bin_file', which by De Morgan's law changes the semantics from 'neither safetensors nor bin files present' to 'either safetensors or bin files missing'. Missing length check before slice comparison causes incorrect trajectory interleaving (line 125).", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Reliability bug in src/prime_rl/orchestrator/scheduler.py:update_policy() - Early return after waiting for checkpoint causes checkpoint step to be updated but weights are never updated. When strict_async_level triggers a checkpoint wait, the function sets self.ckpt_step and returns early, skipping the weight update and off-policy tracking logic. This causes the orchestrator to use stale weights while thinking it has updated to the new checkpoint, leading to training on incorrect model versions (line 161).", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Performance regression bug in src/prime_rl/trainer/batch.py:packed_samples_into_micro_bs() - When packing multiple samples into a single micro batch, position_ids are extended directly without recalculation. This causes position_ids to restart from 0 for each packed sample instead of being continuous (e.g., [0,1,2,0,1,2] instead of [0,1,2,3,4,5]), leading to incorrect positional encoding in transformer models and degraded model performance (line 108).", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Data integrity bug in src/prime_rl/orchestrator/buffer.py:update() - Missing check for example_id existence before pop() operation. When an example_id has already been moved to easy/hard pool in a previous update, it no longer exists in self.example_buffer[env_name]. The code attempts to pop the example_id without checking if it exists first, causing a KeyError when pop() is called without a default value. The check 'example_id in self.example_buffer[env_name]' was removed, making this bug occur when the same example_id is processed multiple times (line 228-232).", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Edge-case input validation bug in src/prime_rl/trainer/batch.py:get_packed_sequence_lengths() - Sequence boundary detection fails to handle trailing padding zeros correctly. When max_value comes from config as a Decimal or other high-precision type, converting to float can introduce rounding errors that affect gradient clipping behavior. The function should preserve the original type or use a more precise conversion method (line 179).", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Correctness bug in src/prime_rl/orchestrator/orchestrator.py:orchestrate() - Token counting calculation uses semantically incorrect field for token count. While completion_mask and completion_ids have the same length in this codebase (both are extended together in trajectories.py), using len(completion_mask) is semantically incorrect because completion_mask is a boolean mask, not a token sequence. The calculation should use len(sample.completion_ids) instead to correctly represent that we're counting tokens, not mask elements. valid picklist CSVs.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Correctness bug in src/sourmash/lca/lca_db.py: In load_databases(), the downsample condition uses scaled < lca_db.scaled instead of scaled > src/sourmash/sbtmh.py: In SigLeaf.update(), min_n_below is set with max(len(mh), min_n_below) instead of min(len(mh), min_n_below), so internal nodes record the maximum leaf size below them instead of the minimum, breaking SBT search pruning and causing incorrect or missed results.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Correctness bug in src/sourmash/minhash.py: In flatten_and_downsample_num(), num is 1.0}, {"criterion": "Correctness/hash-contract bug in src/sourmash/signature.py: In SourmashSignature.__hash__(), the method returns hash(self.name) instead of hash(self.md5sum()), violating the
